title: DGQGSI Introduction
text: DGQGSI provides tools multi-trait selection plant breeding programs using desired-gain–based selection indices. integrates two complementary workflows: DGSI — Desired-Gain Selection Index Optimization: estimates index weights whose realized genetic gains closely match breeder-defined targets (Joukhadar et al., 2024). QGSI — Desired-Gain Quadratic Genomic Selection Index: nonlinear index scores genotypes using linear quadratic GEBV components, capturing trait interactions linear index miss (Cerón-Rojas et al., 2026). key advantage methods breeders specify desired genetic gains directly — “increase yield 1.5 units reducing disease score 1 unit” — rather economic weights, rarely known precision practice.

title: DGQGSI Introduction
text: DGSI finds weight vector b linear index =𝐛⊤𝐗I = \mathbf{b}^\top \mathbf{X} produces realized genetic gains g close possible breeder’s desired gain vector d. algorithm run_desired_gain_index_Joukhadar2024(): Samples candidate desired-gain vectors neighbourhood d. Computes index coefficients b candidate. Evaluates realized gains g selected subset. Retains coefficients minimise deviation g d. search stochastic, multiple independent replicates (n_rep) run best solution across replicates returned.

title: DGQGSI Introduction
text: genotype ii GEBV vector 𝛄i\boldsymbol{\gamma}_i, quadratic index : QGSI_DGi=𝐝⊤𝛄i+𝛄i⊤𝐖d𝛄i\text{QGSI\_DG}_i = \mathbf{d}^\top \boldsymbol{\gamma}_i + \boldsymbol{\gamma}_i^\top \mathbf{W}_d \, \boldsymbol{\gamma}_i d desired gain vector 𝐖d\mathbf{W}_d quadratic interaction matrix built automatically construct_Wd_from_dg(). linear term rewards GEBVs aligned desired direction; quadratic term additionally rewards genotypes combine favourable trait values simultaneously, capturing synergies penalising trade-offs invisible linear index.

title: DGQGSI Introduction
text: package ships two built-datasets. example_pheno.csv contains observed phenotypic means per genotype; example_gebv.csv contains genomic estimated breeding values (GEBVs) genomic prediction model. work six traits across two contrasting groups: desired gain vector dg encodes target improvement per selection cycle. three disease traits, value represents intended reduction; direction flipping managed internally lower_is_better argument, values dg given positive quantities regardless trait direction.
code: library(DGQGSI) library(data.table)  ext   <- system.file("extdata", package = "DGQGSI") pheno <- fread(file.path(ext, "example_pheno.csv")) gebv  <- fread(file.path(ext, "example_gebv.csv"))  traits <- c("YLD", "MY", "MI", "BL", "NBL", "VHB")  dg <- c(YLD = 1.5, MY = 0.5, MI = 0.5, BL = 1, NBL = 1, VHB = 1)

title: DGQGSI Introduction
text: run_dgsi_qgsi_pipeline() main entry point. Setting mode = "" runs DGSI QGSI sequence , merge_outputs = TRUE, combines genotype rankings single comparison table via compare_dg_and_qgsi(). Key arguments: pipeline returns named list. components present depend mode: res$dg_result — full DGSI output run_desired_gain_index_Joukhadar2024(). res$W_d_result — automatically constructed 𝐖d\mathbf{W}_d matrix construct_Wd_from_dg(). res$qgsi_result — full QGSI output run_qgsi_desired_gain(). res$comparison_result — merged comparison table compare_dg_and_qgsi() (mode = "" merge_outputs = TRUE).
code: res <- run_dgsi_qgsi_pipeline(   mode = "both",   init_data  = pheno[, .(GenoID, Family)],   cand_data  = pheno[, c("GenoID", traits), with = FALSE],   ref_data   = pheno[, c("GenoID", traits), with = FALSE],   gebv_data  = gebv,   trait_cols = traits,   dg         = dg,   lower_is_better = c("BL", "NBL", "VHB"),   trait_min_sd    = c(YLD = 0.2, MY = 0.1, MI = 0.1,                       BL  = 0.1, NBL = 0.1, VHB = 0.1),   dg_scale_traits    = FALSE,   qgsi_center_traits = FALSE,   qgsi_scale_traits  = FALSE,   n_iter        = 50,   n_rep         = 3,   merge_outputs = TRUE,   debug         = FALSE )

title: DGQGSI Introduction
text: merged comparison table primary output side--side evaluation. contains, per genotype: DGSI score (DG_SelectionIndex), DGSI selection flag (DG_Selected), linear quadratic QGSI components (LinearDGPart, QuadraticDGPart), final QGSI score (QGSI_DG), QGSI rank (Rank_QGSI_DG), sign-agreement flag (SignSame_DG_QGSI).
code: comp <- res$comparison_result$comparison_table head(comp) #>    GenoID DG_SelectionIndex DG_Selected LinearDGPart QuadraticDGPart  QGSI_DG #>    <char>             <num>      <lgcl>        <num>           <num>    <num> #> 1:   G013          9.095305        TRUE     3.043771       0.8199342 3.863705 #> 2:   G016          7.375457        TRUE     2.934136       0.7698778 3.704014 #> 3:   G029          7.332733        TRUE     3.245327       0.9101123 4.155439 #> 4:   G001          6.995980        TRUE     2.581310       0.4790381 3.060348 #> 5:   G007          6.072997        TRUE     1.752643       0.2413784 1.994021 #> 6:   G025          5.851942        TRUE     1.849823       1.1382887 2.988112 #>    Rank_QGSI_DG SignSame_DG_QGSI #>           <num>           <lgcl> #> 1:            2             TRUE #> 2:            3             TRUE #> 3:            1             TRUE #> 4:            4             TRUE #> 5:            7             TRUE #> 6:            5             TRUE

title: DGQGSI Introduction
text: Sorting DG_SelectionIndex (descending) reveals genotypes whose multi-trait phenotypic profile best matches optimised linear index weights:

title: DGQGSI Introduction
text: Sorting Rank_QGSI_DG (ascending) highlights genotypes combine favourable GEBVs across multiple traits simultaneously. quadratic term rewards candidates good several fronts :

what: Rank agreement between DGSI and QGSI
title: DGQGSI Introduction
text: Spearman correlation DGSI score ranking QGSI rank summarises overall agreement across candidates: high correlation indicates methods largely agree genotypes advance. correlation lower, genotypes score high one method deserve closer inspection: tend strong trait interactions quadratic term captures. Pearson Spearman correlations computed compare_dg_and_qgsi() also available directly:
code: cor_val <- cor(   rank(-comp$DG_SelectionIndex),   comp$Rank_QGSI_DG,   method = "spearman" ) cat(sprintf("Spearman rank correlation (DGSI vs QGSI): %.3f\n", cor_val)) #> Spearman rank correlation (DGSI vs QGSI): 0.927 res$comparison_result$correlation_summary #>                      Comparison  Pearson Spearman     N #>                          <char>    <num>    <num> <int> #> 1: DG_SelectionIndex vs QGSI_DG 0.921576 0.927475    30

title: DGQGSI Introduction
text: DGSI result contains several diagnostics beyond ranked genotype table: selection_summary table reports mean SD trait full candidate set selected subset, together realized gain expressed SD units. Values 0.9 mean_replicate_cor indicate good agreement across replicates.
code: # Optimised index coefficients res$dg_result$optimized_b #>        YLD         MY         MI         BL        NBL        VHB  #>  2.3422139 -1.0119477  0.5052116  1.6742415  3.2816720  1.4745092  # Realized gains in the transformed trait space res$dg_result$realized_g #> YLD  MY  MI  BL NBL VHB  #>   0   0   0   0   0   0  # Objective value of the best replicate (lower = better match to dg) res$dg_result$best_q #> [1] 6  # Mean pairwise correlation among replicate index vectors (stability check) res$dg_result$mean_replicate_cor #> [1] 0.7722059  # Trait-level summary: all candidates vs. selected subset res$dg_result$selection_summary #>     Trait    Mean_All Mean_Selected    SD_All SD_Selected Realized_Gain_SD #>    <char>       <num>         <num>     <num>       <num>            <num> #> 1:    YLD  0.49133893    0.49133893 1.0801658   1.0801658                0 #> 2:     MY  0.15544154    0.15544154 0.8077812   0.8077812                0 #> 3:     MI  0.21967789    0.21967789 0.6253735   0.6253735                0 #> 4:     BL  0.14404814    0.14404814 0.9487245   0.9487245                0 #> 5:    NBL -0.03033278   -0.03033278 0.6348214   0.6348214                0 #> 6:    VHB -0.02779384   -0.02779384 0.9537685   0.9537685                0

title: DGQGSI Introduction
text: QGSI result separates linear quadratic components per genotype: component-level summary (mean, SD, range) also available:

title: DGQGSI Introduction
text: workflows can also called independently finer control needed.

title: DGQGSI Introduction
text: run_desired_gain_index_Joukhadar2024() supports two selection strategies via select_mode: "top_n" — selects n_select highest-ranking genotypes. "trait_thresholds" — selects genotypes meeting minimum per-trait thresholds (trait_min_sd), optional fallback top-N genotype passes.
code: dgsi_res <- run_desired_gain_index_Joukhadar2024(   init_data       = pheno[, .(GenoID, Family)],   cand_data       = pheno[, c("GenoID", traits), with = FALSE],   ref_data        = pheno[, c("GenoID", traits), with = FALSE],   trait_cols      = traits,   dg              = dg,   lower_is_better = c("BL", "NBL", "VHB"),   select_mode     = "trait_thresholds",   trait_min_sd    = c(YLD = 0.2, MY = 0.1, MI = 0.1,                       BL  = 0.1, NBL = 0.1, VHB = 0.1),   scale_traits    = FALSE,   n_iter          = 200,   n_rep           = 10,   debug           = FALSE )  head(dgsi_res$ranked_geno) dgsi_res$selection_summary

title: DGQGSI Introduction
text: run_qgsi_desired_gain() requires GEBVs desired-gain vector. full 𝐖d\mathbf{W}_d matrix can supplied via W_d; otherwise diagonal matrix built quadratic_diag_weights, captures squared trait effects without cross-trait interactions.
code: qgsi_res <- run_qgsi_desired_gain(   init_data         = pheno[, .(GenoID, Family)],   gebv_data         = gebv[, c("GenoID", traits), with = FALSE],   trait_cols        = traits,   dg                = dg,   lower_is_better   = c("BL", "NBL", "VHB"),   center_traits     = FALSE,   scale_traits      = FALSE,   return_components = TRUE,   debug             = FALSE )  head(qgsi_res$ranked_geno) qgsi_res$component_summary

title: DGQGSI Introduction
text: construct_Wd_from_dg() derives 𝐖d\mathbf{W}_d desired gain vector empirical trait correlation structure GEBV data. supports four construction strategies via method argument: Positive -diagonal entries indicate simultaneous improvement two traits rewarded quadratic term. diagonal entries encode per-trait squared contribution, scaled magnitude desired gain. empirical trait correlation matrix used build 𝐖d\mathbf{W}_d also returned: mode = "qgsi" mode = "" pipeline, 𝐖d\mathbf{W}_d constructed automatically default settings unless pre-built matrix passed via W_d.

title: DGQGSI Introduction
text: run DGSI QGSI separate calls, compare_dg_and_qgsi() merges genotype ID computes rank-difference diagnostics correlation summaries: sort_by argument accepts "DG_rank", "QGSI_rank", "DG", "QGSI", "none". Note "DG_rank" requires Rank_SelectionIndex present dg_result$ranked_geno; use "DG" sort score running select_mode = "trait_thresholds".
code: merged <- compare_dg_and_qgsi(   dg_result                = dgsi_res,   qgsi_result              = qgsi_res,   id_col                   = "GenoID",   compute_rank_differences = TRUE,   add_correlation_summary  = TRUE,   sort_by                  = "DG",   debug                    = FALSE )  head(merged$comparison_table) merged$correlation_summary

title: DGQGSI Introduction
text: Trait scaling. traits different raw scales, set dg_scale_traits = TRUE DGSI qgsi_scale_traits = TRUE QGSI. scaling, dg values interpreted standard-deviation units. traits already comparable, leave flags FALSE (default). Calibrating desired gains. relative magnitudes values dg determine emphasis placed trait. Calibrate targets historical rates genetic gain germplasm ensure biologically achievable single selection cycle. lower_is_better traits. Include every trait decrease breeding goal. Internally, function multiplies columns −1-1 objectives point direction. Always specify dg values positive quantities regardless trait direction. Optimisation stability. DGSI search stochastic. Increase n_rep n_iter stable results cost computation time. exploratory work, n_iter = 50 n_rep = 3 adequate. final selection decisions, n_iter = 200 n_rep = 10 higher recommended. mean_replicate_cor diagnostic provides quick stability check: values 0.9 indicate good agreement across replicates. Choosing DGSI QGSI. Debug mode. Set debug = TRUE function print intermediate matrices, gain vectors, sorting decisions step.

title: DGQGSI
text: DGQGSI provides tools multi-trait selection plant breeding programs using desired-gain–based selection indices. integrates two complementary approaches: Desired-Gain Selection Index Optimization (DGSI) Inspired Joukhadar et al. (2024), workflow estimates index weights produce realized genetic gains close breeder-defined desired gains. Desired-Gain Quadratic Genomic Selection Index (QGSI) Based Cerón-Rojas et al. (2026), nonlinear genomic index incorporates linear quadratic trait components capture interactions among traits classical linear index miss. key advantage methods breeders specify desired genetic gains directly — “increase yield 1.5 units reducing disease score 1 unit” — rather economic weights, rarely known precision practice. package also provides: automatic construction quadratic desired-gain matrix W_d high-level pipeline wrapper running one workflows comparison helper merges DGSI QGSI outputs genotype

title: DGQGSI
text: Modern crop breeding programs must improve multiple traits simultaneously: yield, stress tolerance, disease resistance, end-use quality. Classical selection indices solve assigning economic weights trait, practice breeders rarely know true economic value unit change , say, disease severity score relative grain yield. intuitive alternative specify desired genetic gains directly. DGQGSI formalises idea providing tools : optimise selection indices using breeder-defined desired gains integrate genomic predictions (GEBVs) index incorporate nonlinear trait interactions quadratic component compare alternative selection strategies side side

title: DGQGSI
text: DGSI finds weight vector b linear index =𝐛⊤𝐗I = \mathbf{b}^\top \mathbf{X} produces realized gains g matching breeder’s desired gain vector d. algorithm run_desired_gain_index_Joukhadar2024(): Samples candidate desired-gain vectors around d. Computes index coefficients b candidate. Evaluates realized gains g selected subset. Retains weights minimise deviation g d. search stochastic, multiple independent replicates run best solution across replicates returned, together replicate-stability diagnostic (mean_replicate_cor).

title: DGQGSI
text: genotype ii GEBV vector 𝛄i\boldsymbol{\gamma}_i, quadratic index : QGSI_DGi=𝐝⊤𝛄i+𝛄i⊤𝐖d𝛄i\mathrm{QGSI\_DG}_i = \mathbf{d}^\top \boldsymbol{\gamma}_i + \boldsymbol{\gamma}_i^\top \mathbf{W}_d \, \boldsymbol{\gamma}_i d desired gain vector 𝐖d\mathbf{W}_d quadratic interaction matrix constructed automatically construct_Wd_from_dg(). linear term rewards GEBVs aligned desired direction; quadratic term additionally rewards genotypes combine favourable trait values simultaneously, capturing synergies penalising trade-offs invisible linear index.

title: DGQGSI
code: Phenotypes / GEBVs         │         ▼ Desired Gain Vector (d)         │         ├───────────────┐         │               │         ▼               ▼ DGSI Optimization     QGSI Computation         │               │         ▼               ▼ Selection Index       QGSI Score         │               │         └───────┬───────┘                 ▼        Compare Genotype Rankings

title: DGQGSI
text: Full documentation tutorials available : https://FAkohoue.github.io/DGQGSI/

title: DGQGSI
text: pipeline returns named list:
code: library(DGQGSI) library(data.table)  traits <- c("YLD", "MY", "MI", "BL", "NBL", "VHB")  # Desired gains: positive values for all traits regardless of direction. # lower_is_better handles sign flipping internally. dg <- c(YLD = 1.5, MY = 0.5, MI = 0.5, BL = 1, NBL = 1, VHB = 1)  ext   <- system.file("extdata", package = "DGQGSI") pheno <- fread(file.path(ext, "example_pheno.csv")) gebv  <- fread(file.path(ext, "example_gebv.csv"))  res <- run_dgsi_qgsi_pipeline(   mode             = "both",   init_data        = pheno[, .(GenoID, Family)],   cand_data        = pheno[, c("GenoID", traits), with = FALSE],   ref_data         = pheno[, c("GenoID", traits), with = FALSE],   gebv_data        = gebv[, c("GenoID", traits), with = FALSE],   trait_cols       = traits,   dg               = dg,   lower_is_better  = c("BL", "NBL", "VHB"),   trait_min_sd     = c(YLD = 0.2, MY = 0.1, MI = 0.1,                        BL  = 0.1, NBL = 0.1, VHB = 0.1),   dg_scale_traits    = FALSE,   qgsi_center_traits = FALSE,   qgsi_scale_traits  = FALSE,   merge_outputs    = TRUE,   debug            = FALSE )  # Side-by-side DGSI and QGSI rankings head(res$comparison_result$comparison_table)  # Rank agreement between the two methods res$comparison_result$correlation_summary

what: Merge desired-gain index and desired-gain QGSI outputs into one comparison table — compare_dg_and_qgsi
title: Merge desired-gain index and desired-gain QGSI outputs into one comparison table — compare_dg_and_qgsi
text: Merges outputs run_desired_gain_index_Joukhadar2024() run_qgsi_desired_gain() one genotype-level comparison table ID. helper function intended situations desired-gain selection index workflow desired-gain QGSI workflow run overlapping sets genotypes, user wants compare: genotype scores methods, genotype ranks methods, selected versus non-selected status DGSI workflow, agreement disagreement ranking systems.

title: Merge desired-gain index and desired-gain QGSI outputs into one comparison table — compare_dg_and_qgsi
code: compare_dg_and_qgsi(   dg_result,   qgsi_result,   id_col = "GenoID",   dg_cols = c(id_col, "SelectionIndex", "Selected"),   qgsi_cols = c(id_col, "LinearDGPart", "QuadraticDGPart", "QGSI_DG", "Rank_QGSI_DG"),   include_metadata = TRUE,   compute_rank_differences = TRUE,   add_correlation_summary = TRUE,   sort_by = c("DG_rank", "QGSI_rank", "DG", "QGSI", "none"),   debug = TRUE )

title: Merge desired-gain index and desired-gain QGSI outputs into one comparison table — compare_dg_and_qgsi
text: dg_result result list returned run_desired_gain_index_Joukhadar2024(). function expects least ranked_geno component containing DGSI output table. qgsi_result result list returned run_qgsi_desired_gain(). function expects least ranked_geno component containing QGSI output table. id_col Character string naming genotype identifier column used merge DGSI QGSI outputs. Default "GenoID". column must present dg_result$ranked_geno qgsi_result$ranked_geno. dg_cols Character vector giving columns extract dg_result$ranked_geno. default, includes genotype identifier, DG selection index, selected/non-selected flag. Additional metadata columns family population can also requested . qgsi_cols Character vector giving columns extract qgsi_result$ranked_geno. default, includes genotype identifier, QGSI linear quadratic components, final QGSI score, QGSI rank. include_metadata Logical retained API consistency future use. present, metadata included whenever explicitly requested dg_cols qgsi_cols. Default TRUE. compute_rank_differences Logical. TRUE, function computes diagnostic columns comparing DGSI QGSI ranks, including: RankDiff_DG_minus_QGSI AbsRankDiff_DG_vs_QGSI SignSame_DG_QGSI score columns available add_correlation_summary Logical. TRUE, function computes summary table containing Pearson Spearman correlations : DG selection index QGSI score DG rank QGSI rank whenever required columns available. sort_by Character string specifying merged output table ordered. One : "DG_rank": sort DG rank ascending "QGSI_rank": sort QGSI rank ascending "DG": sort DG score descending "QGSI": sort QGSI score descending "none": apply additional sorting merging debug Logical. TRUE, function prints progress diagnostic messages, including missing requested columns, computed correlation summaries, final sorting rule applied.

title: Merge desired-gain index and desired-gain QGSI outputs into one comparison table — compare_dg_and_qgsi
text: list containing: comparison_table data.table containing merged genotype-level outputs DGSI QGSI, optionally augmented rank-difference diagnostics. correlation_summary data.table containing Pearson Spearman correlations DGSI QGSI scores /ranks add_correlation_summary = TRUE; otherwise NULL.

title: Merge desired-gain index and desired-gain QGSI outputs into one comparison table — compare_dg_and_qgsi
text: function: extracts selected columns dg_result$ranked_geno; extracts selected columns qgsi_result$ranked_geno; renames overlapping DG columns clarity; merges tables id_col; optionally computes rank-difference diagnostics; optionally computes correlation summaries DGSI QGSI scores /ranks; optionally sorts final merged table according chosen ranking criterion. especially useful downstream interpretation, allows side--side comparison : DG_SelectionIndex QGSI_DG respective ranks magnitude rank disagreement requested columns found either result object, skipped debug message rather causing immediate failure, long identifier column available.

title: Merge desired-gain index and desired-gain QGSI outputs into one comparison table — compare_dg_and_qgsi
code: trait_cols <- c("YLD", "MY", "MI", "BL", "NBL", "VHB")  dg <- c(   YLD = 1.5,   MY  = 0.5,   MI  = 0.5,   BL  = 1.0,   NBL = 1.0,   VHB = 1.0 )  trait_min_sd <- c(   YLD = 0.2,   MY  = 0.1,   MI  = 0.1,   BL  = 0.1,   NBL = 0.1,   VHB = 0.1 )  ext <- system.file("extdata", package = "DGQGSI") pheno <- data.table::fread(file.path(ext, "example_pheno.csv")) gebv  <- data.table::fread(file.path(ext, "example_gebv.csv"))  dg_res <- run_desired_gain_index_Joukhadar2024(   init_data = pheno[, .(GenoID, Family)],   cand_data = pheno[, c("GenoID", trait_cols), with = FALSE],   ref_data = pheno[, c("GenoID", trait_cols), with = FALSE],   trait_cols = trait_cols,   dg = dg,   lower_is_better = c("BL", "NBL", "VHB"),   select_mode = "trait_thresholds",   trait_min_sd = trait_min_sd,   scale_traits = FALSE,   debug = FALSE )  qgsi_res <- run_qgsi_desired_gain(   init_data = pheno[, .(GenoID, Family)],   gebv_data = gebv[, c("GenoID", trait_cols), with = FALSE],   trait_cols = trait_cols,   dg = dg,   lower_is_better = c("BL", "NBL", "VHB"),   center_traits = FALSE,   scale_traits = FALSE,   debug = FALSE )  merged <- compare_dg_and_qgsi(   dg_result = dg_res,   qgsi_result = qgsi_res,   id_col = "GenoID",   compute_rank_differences = TRUE,   add_correlation_summary = TRUE,   sort_by = "DG_rank",   debug = FALSE )  head(merged$comparison_table) #>    GenoID DG_SelectionIndex DG_Selected LinearDGPart QuadraticDGPart  QGSI_DG #>    <char>             <num>      <lgcl>        <num>           <num>    <num> #> 1:   G013          9.095305       FALSE     3.043771       2.4040859 5.447857 #> 2:   G016          7.375457       FALSE     2.934136       2.9481011 5.882237 #> 3:   G029          7.332733       FALSE     3.245327       3.4324322 6.677759 #> 4:   G001          6.995980        TRUE     2.581310       1.2789864 3.860296 #> 5:   G007          6.072997       FALSE     1.752643       0.8931555 2.645798 #> 6:   G025          5.851942       FALSE     1.849823       4.6185705 6.468394 #>    Rank_QGSI_DG SignSame_DG_QGSI #>           <num>           <lgcl> #> 1:            4             TRUE #> 2:            3             TRUE #> 3:            1             TRUE #> 4:            5             TRUE #> 5:           11             TRUE #> 6:            2             TRUE merged$correlation_summary #>                      Comparison   Pearson  Spearman     N #>                          <char>     <num>     <num> <int> #> 1: DG_SelectionIndex vs QGSI_DG 0.8510171 0.8874305    30

what: Construct a quadratic desired-gain matrix W_d automatically for QGSI — construct_Wd_from_dg
title: Construct a quadratic desired-gain matrix W_d automatically for QGSI — construct_Wd_from_dg
text: Builds symmetric quadratic desired-gain matrix desired-gain QGSI formulation: $$QGSI\_DG_i = d' \hat{\gamma}_i + \hat{\gamma}_i' W_d \hat{\gamma}_i$$ helper function provides flexible way construct quadratic matrix W_d desired-gain vector observed trait relationships GEBV dataset.

title: Construct a quadratic desired-gain matrix W_d automatically for QGSI — construct_Wd_from_dg
code: construct_Wd_from_dg(   gebv_data,   trait_cols,   dg,   lower_is_better = NULL,   center_traits = TRUE,   scale_traits = TRUE,   impute_missing = TRUE,   method = c("hybrid", "diag_only", "dg_outer", "corr_weighted"),   base_diag = NULL,   lambda_diag = 1,   lambda_outer = 0.5,   lambda_corr = 1,   corr_power = 1,   offdiag_shrink = 1,   positive_diagonal_only = TRUE,   normalize = c("trace", "max_abs", "none"),   debug = TRUE )

title: Construct a quadratic desired-gain matrix W_d automatically for QGSI — construct_Wd_from_dg
text: gebv_data data.frame data.table containing trait GEBVs. row represent one genotype, columns listed trait_cols contain numeric coercible--numeric trait GEBVs. trait_cols Character vector giving names trait columns use constructing W_d. columns must exist gebv_data. order determines order used returned matrix. dg Named numeric desired-gain vector, names matching trait_cols. desired gains used build outer-product correlation-weighted components W_d, define default diagonal weights base_diag = NULL. lower_is_better Optional character vector trait names smaller values favorable. traits internally multiplied -1 traits oriented common favorable direction correlations matrix components computed. center_traits Logical. TRUE, center selected GEBV columns subtracting means deriving trait relationships. useful trait GEBVs already centered around zero. scale_traits Logical. TRUE, divide selected trait column standard deviation optional centering. useful traits different scales want correlation structure based standardized variables. impute_missing Logical. TRUE, missing values imputed mean corresponding trait column. FALSE, function stops error missing values present selected trait columns. method Character string specifying W_d constructed. One : "hybrid" "diag_only" "dg_outer" "corr_weighted" base_diag Optional named numeric vector giving baseline diagonal weights. Names must match trait_cols. NULL, function uses abs(dg) default diagonal weights. lambda_diag Numeric multiplier applied diagonal component W_d. Larger values increase contribution squared trait effects. lambda_outer Numeric multiplier applied desired-gain outer product component. Larger values increase contribution pairwise trait interactions implied desired-gain vector alone. lambda_corr Numeric multiplier applied correlation-weighted component. Larger values increase influence empirical trait correlation structure W_d. corr_power Numeric exponent applied absolute trait correlations building correlation-weighted component. Values greater 1 emphasize strong correlations heavily; values 0 1 reduce contrast among correlation magnitudes. offdiag_shrink Numeric shrinkage factor applied -diagonal elements W_d. Must 0 1. value 1 leaves -diagonal elements unchanged; smaller values weaken cross-trait interaction terms. positive_diagonal_only Logical. TRUE, negative diagonal entries replaced zero construction. ensures non-negative diagonal contributions final matrix. normalize Character string controlling normalization final matrix. One : "trace": divide W_d sum absolute diagonal values; "max_abs": divide W_d maximum absolute entry; "none": normalize. debug Logical. TRUE, print progress diagnostic messages matrix construction.

title: Construct a quadratic desired-gain matrix W_d automatically for QGSI — construct_Wd_from_dg
text: list containing: W_d final symmetric quadratic desired-gain matrix. trait_matrix_used data.table containing transformed GEBV matrix actually used derive trait relationships optional imputation, direction flipping, centering, scaling. cor_matrix empirical trait correlation matrix computed transformed GEBV matrix. diag_component diagonal component used constructing W_d. outer_component desired-gain outer-product component. corr_component correlation-weighted component. method construction method used.

title: Construct a quadratic desired-gain matrix W_d automatically for QGSI — construct_Wd_from_dg
text: matrix W_d governs quadratic part desired-gain QGSI. can interpreted controlling strongly: squared trait effects contribute index, pairwise trait interactions contribute index, correlation structure among traits influences quadratic term. function supports four strategies constructing W_d: "diag_only" Builds purely diagonal matrix, quadratic term contains squared trait effects cross-trait interactions. "dg_outer" Uses outer product desired-gain vector, \(d d'\), construct W_d. introduces pairwise interaction terms based desired gains. "corr_weighted" Constructs W_d weighting desired-gain outer product empirical trait correlation matrix derived transformed GEBV data. allows quadratic matrix reflect observed relationships among traits. "hybrid" Combines diagonal component, outer-product component, correlation-weighted component. flexible option generally good default squared effects trait interactions interest. construction workflow : convert selected GEBV columns numeric; optionally impute missing values; optionally flip traits lower_is_better; optionally center /scale GEBV matrix; compute trait correlation matrix transformed GEBV matrix; construct diagonal, outer-product, correlation-weighted components; combine components according method lambda weights; optionally shrink -diagonal terms; enforce symmetry optional diagonal positivity; optionally normalize final matrix. trait GEBVs already centered scaled, may wish use: center_traits = FALSE scale_traits = FALSE returned W_d can passed directly run_qgsi_desired_gain().

title: Construct a quadratic desired-gain matrix W_d automatically for QGSI — construct_Wd_from_dg
text: Cerón-Rojas JJ, Montesinos-López OA, Montesinos-López , et al. (2026). Nonlinear genomic selection index accelerates multi-trait crop improvement. Nature Communications, 17:1991. doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69890-3

what: Run a desired-gain selection index with optional trait scaling and threshold-based or top-N selection — run_desired_gain_index_Joukhadar2024
title: Run a desired-gain selection index with optional trait scaling and threshold-based or top-N selection — run_desired_gain_index_Joukhadar2024
text: Implements desired-gain selection index optimization strategy inspired Joukhadar et al. (2024). function searches index coefficients produce realized multi-trait gains close user-defined desired-gain vector ranks genotypes using best replicate.

title: Run a desired-gain selection index with optional trait scaling and threshold-based or top-N selection — run_desired_gain_index_Joukhadar2024
code: run_desired_gain_index_Joukhadar2024(   init_data,   cand_data,   trait_cols,   ref_data = NULL,   id_col = "GenoID",   scale_traits = FALSE,   lower_is_better = NULL,   G = NULL,   dg,   select_mode = c("top_n", "trait_thresholds"),   n_select = 100,   trait_min_sd = NULL,   fallback_to_top_n = TRUE,   n_iter = 1000,   n_rep = 20,   sd_scale = 1,   seed = 42,   ridge_P = 1e-06,   ridge_M = 1e-06,   debug = TRUE,   return_all_reps = TRUE )

title: Run a desired-gain selection index with optional trait scaling and threshold-based or top-N selection — run_desired_gain_index_Joukhadar2024
text: init_data data.frame data.table containing genotype identifiers metadata want preserve final output. Typical columns may include GenoID, family, group, descriptive information. function returns table augmented final SelectionIndex Selected columns. cand_data data.frame data.table containing candidate trait values used build desired-gain index. must contain id_col columns listed trait_cols. row represents one genotype trait column numeric coercible numeric. trait_cols Character vector giving names trait columns include index. names must present cand_data ref_data (cand_data alone ref_data = NULL). order trait_cols defines order used dg, trait_min_sd, P, G. ref_data Optional data.frame data.table used reference population scaling traits estimating phenotypic correlation matrix P , necessary, genetic covariance matrix G. NULL, cand_data used reference. id_col Character string naming genotype identifier column used align init_data cand_data. Default "GenoID". function requires column exists init_data cand_data. scale_traits Logical. TRUE, trait columns candidate reference data centered scaled using mean standard deviation ref_data. Use traits different raw scales. FALSE, function assumes traits already comparable scale, SD units. lower_is_better Optional character vector naming traits smaller values favorable. traits internally multiplied -1 traits oriented common “higher better” direction optimization. G Optional square genetic covariance matrix among traits. dimension must length(trait_cols) x length(trait_cols). NULL, function estimates G cov(ref_mat) reference trait matrix. dg Named numeric vector desired gains, names matching trait_cols. value represents desired response one trait trait space used function. best interpretability, dg scale transformed trait values. select_mode Character string specifying selected subset determined. One : "top_n": selects n_select highest-ranked genotypes final index; "trait_thresholds": selects genotypes meeting minimum trait thresholds transformed trait space. n_select Integer giving number genotypes select select_mode = "top_n". Ignored select_mode = "trait_thresholds" unless fallback triggered. trait_min_sd Named numeric vector minimum acceptable trait values used select_mode = "trait_thresholds". Names must match trait_cols. Thresholds applied direction flipping optional scaling, specified transformed scale. fallback_to_top_n Logical. Relevant select_mode = "trait_thresholds". TRUE genotype passes thresholds, function falls back selecting top n_select genotypes final index. FALSE, function stops error. n_iter Integer giving number optimization iterations performed within replicate. Larger values increase search effort also increase computation time. n_rep Integer giving number independent optimization replicates. Replicates useful search stochastic, different replicates may converge slightly different solutions. sd_scale Numeric factor controlling perturbation scale used sampling candidate desired-gain vectors around current best solution. Larger values increase exploration; smaller values keep search local around current optimum. seed Integer random seed used make stochastic optimization reproducible. ridge_P Numeric ridge penalty added phenotypic correlation matrix P inversion. can improve numerical stability P close singular. ridge_M Numeric ridge penalty added inner matrix M coefficient computation. can improve stability cases highly correlated traits poorly conditioned matrices. debug Logical. TRUE, function prints progress diagnostic messages execution. helpful understanding function troubleshooting. return_all_reps Logical. TRUE, returned object includes full list replicate-level optimization results. FALSE, summary corresponding best replicate returned.

title: Run a desired-gain selection index with optional trait scaling and threshold-based or top-N selection — run_desired_gain_index_Joukhadar2024
text: list containing: dg desired-gain vector used analysis. trait_min_sd threshold vector used select_mode = "trait_thresholds", otherwise NULL. optimized_d best sampled desired-gain vector winning replicate. optimized_b optimized index coefficient vector winning replicate. realized_g realized gains achieved selected subset transformed trait space. best_q objective value best replicate. Lower values indicate closer match realized desired gains. q_trace iteration-wise objective values best replicate. mean_replicate_cor Mean pairwise correlation among replicate index vectors, used simple replicate-stability diagnostic. best_replicate index replicate best objective value. ranked_geno data.table containing init_data plus final SelectionIndex Selected columns, sorted decreasing SelectionIndex. selection_summary data.table summarizing mean trait values realized gains candidates versus selected subset. selected_geno Subset ranked_geno Selected == TRUE. non_selected_geno Subset ranked_geno Selected == FALSE. all_reps Optional list replicate results return_all_reps = TRUE.

title: Run a desired-gain selection index with optional trait scaling and threshold-based or top-N selection — run_desired_gain_index_Joukhadar2024
text: workflow: aligns init_data cand_data id_col; optionally flips traits lower_is_better objectives direction; optionally scales traits using ref_data; computes phenotypic correlation matrix P genetic covariance matrix G; repeatedly samples candidate desired-gain vectors, computes index coefficients, evaluates realized gains selected set, keeps best solution according goodness--fit criterion. function selection-oriented. return one index value per genotype, also determines selected subset based either: highest index values (select_mode = "top_n"), user-defined minimum trait thresholds (select_mode = "trait_thresholds"). trait values already expressed comparable standardized units (example SD units), set scale_traits = FALSE. raw trait values used, scale_traits = TRUE generally appropriate.

title: Run a desired-gain selection index with optional trait scaling and threshold-based or top-N selection — run_desired_gain_index_Joukhadar2024
text: Joukhadar R, Li Y, Thistlethwaite R, Forrest KL, Tibbits JF, Trethowan R, Hayden MJ (2024). Optimising desired gain indices maximise selection response. Frontiers Plant Science, 15:1337388. doi:10.3389/fpls.2024.1337388

title: Run a desired-gain selection index with optional trait scaling and threshold-based or top-N selection — run_desired_gain_index_Joukhadar2024
code: trait_cols <- c("YLD", "MY", "MI", "BL", "NBL", "VHB")  dg <- c(   YLD = 1.5,   MY  = 0.5,   MI  = 0.5,   BL  = 1.0,   NBL = 1.0,   VHB = 1.0 )  trait_min_sd <- c(   YLD = 0.2,   MY  = 0.1,   MI  = 0.1,   BL  = 0.1,   NBL = 0.1,   VHB = 0.1 )  ext <- system.file("extdata", package = "DGQGSI") pheno <- data.table::fread(file.path(ext, "example_pheno.csv"))  res <- run_desired_gain_index_Joukhadar2024(   init_data = pheno[, .(GenoID, Family)],   cand_data = pheno[, c("GenoID", trait_cols), with = FALSE],   ref_data = pheno[, c("GenoID", trait_cols), with = FALSE],   trait_cols = trait_cols,   dg = dg,   lower_is_better = c("BL", "NBL", "VHB"),   select_mode = "trait_thresholds",   trait_min_sd = trait_min_sd,   scale_traits = FALSE,   debug = FALSE )  head(res$ranked_geno) #>    GenoID Family SelectionIndex Selected #>    <char> <char>          <num>   <lgcl> #> 1:   G013     F3       9.095305    FALSE #> 2:   G016     F1       7.375457    FALSE #> 3:   G029     F4       7.332733    FALSE #> 4:   G001     F1       6.995980     TRUE #> 5:   G007     F2       6.072997    FALSE #> 6:   G025     F5       5.851942    FALSE res$selection_summary #>     Trait    Mean_All Mean_Selected    SD_All SD_Selected Realized_Gain_SD #>    <char>       <num>         <num>     <num>       <num>            <num> #> 1:    YLD  0.49133893     1.5881848 1.0801658          NA        1.0154421 #> 2:     MY  0.15544154     1.1595565 0.8077812          NA        1.2430532 #> 3:     MI  0.21967789     0.1464351 0.6253735          NA       -0.1171185 #> 4:     BL  0.14404814     0.8116349 0.9487245          NA        0.7036677 #> 5:    NBL -0.03033278     0.8645213 0.6348214          NA        1.4096154 #> 6:    VHB -0.02779384     0.1217989 0.9537685          NA        0.1568439

what: Run a full desired-gain index and desired-gain QGSI pipeline — run_dgsi_qgsi_pipeline
title: Run a full desired-gain index and desired-gain QGSI pipeline — run_dgsi_qgsi_pipeline
text: High-level wrapper runs one desired-gain selection index optimization workflow desired-gain quadratic genomic selection index (QGSI) workflow, optional merged comparison output. function orchestrates full DGQGSI pipeline, allowing users : compute desired-gain selection index following Joukhadar et al. (2024), compute desired-gain quadratic genomic selection index (QGSI) following Cerón-Rojas et al. (2026), automatically construct quadratic desired-gain matrix W_d needed, generate merged comparison table two methods. function acts convenient wrapper coordinates multiple package functions, ensuring consistent input alignment reducing repetitive code running complete selection-index analyses.

title: Run a full desired-gain index and desired-gain QGSI pipeline — run_dgsi_qgsi_pipeline
code: run_dgsi_qgsi_pipeline(   mode = c("both", "dg", "qgsi"),   init_data,   trait_cols,   dg,   id_col = "GenoID",   cand_data = NULL,   ref_data = NULL,   G = NULL,   select_mode = c("top_n", "trait_thresholds"),   n_select = 100,   trait_min_sd = NULL,   fallback_to_top_n = TRUE,   n_iter = 1000,   n_rep = 20,   sd_scale = 1,   seed = 42,   ridge_P = 1e-06,   ridge_M = 1e-06,   dg_scale_traits = FALSE,   gebv_data = NULL,   W_d = NULL,   quadratic_diag_weights = NULL,   qgsi_center_traits = FALSE,   qgsi_scale_traits = FALSE,   qgsi_impute_missing = TRUE,   W_method = c("hybrid", "diag_only", "dg_outer", "corr_weighted"),   W_base_diag = NULL,   W_lambda_diag = 1,   W_lambda_outer = 0.5,   W_lambda_corr = 1,   W_corr_power = 1,   W_offdiag_shrink = 1,   W_positive_diagonal_only = TRUE,   W_normalize = c("trace", "max_abs", "none"),   lower_is_better = NULL,   merge_outputs = TRUE,   compare_sort_by = c("DG_rank", "QGSI_rank", "DG", "QGSI", "none"),   debug = TRUE )

title: Run a full desired-gain index and desired-gain QGSI pipeline — run_dgsi_qgsi_pipeline
text: mode Character string specifying workflow run. One "", "dg", "qgsi". init_data data.frame data.table containing genotype identifiers metadata (example GenoID, family, population). table preserved appended index results. trait_cols Character vector trait names used DG QGSI calculations. columns must exist candidate trait data (cand_data) /GEBV data (gebv_data). dg Named numeric desired-gain vector whose names correspond trait_cols. vector defines breeding objectives used DGSI QGSI calculations. id_col Character genotype identifier column used align input tables. Default "GenoID". cand_data Optional data.frame data.table containing candidate trait values desired-gain index workflow. ref_data Optional reference dataset used scaling covariance estimation desired-gain index workflow. G Optional genetic covariance matrix used desired-gain index optimization. NULL, estimated reference data. select_mode Selection strategy used DG workflow. One "top_n" "trait_thresholds". n_select Integer specifying number selected genotypes select_mode = "top_n". trait_min_sd Named numeric vector specifying minimum selection thresholds select_mode = "trait_thresholds". Values expressed standard deviation units. fallback_to_top_n Logical. TRUE, function falls back top-N selection threshold selection returns zero genotypes. n_iter Integer number optimization iterations used desired-gain index search algorithm. n_rep Integer number optimization replicates. sd_scale Numeric perturbation scale applied candidate desired-gain vectors optimization. seed Integer random seed controlling reproducibility optimization procedure. ridge_P Numeric ridge penalty added phenotypic correlation matrix used desired-gain index calculation. ridge_M Numeric ridge penalty added inner matrix used coefficient computation. dg_scale_traits Logical. TRUE, traits centered scaled computing desired-gain index. gebv_data Optional data.frame data.table containing genotype-level GEBVs used QGSI computation. W_d Optional quadratic desired-gain matrix. NULL, matrix automatically generated using construct_Wd_from_dg(). quadratic_diag_weights Optional named numeric vector used construct diagonal W_d matrix full matrix provided. qgsi_center_traits Logical. TRUE, GEBV matrix centered computing QGSI. qgsi_scale_traits Logical. TRUE, GEBV matrix scaled computing QGSI. qgsi_impute_missing Logical. TRUE, missing GEBV values imputed trait means computing QGSI. W_method Method used automatically construct W_d. Passed construct_Wd_from_dg(). Options include "hybrid", "diag_only", "dg_outer", "corr_weighted". W_base_diag Optional baseline diagonal weights automatic W_d construction. W_lambda_diag Weight applied diagonal component building W_d. W_lambda_outer Weight applied desired-gain outer-product component W_d. W_lambda_corr Weight applied correlation-weighted component W_d. W_corr_power Exponent applied trait correlations constructing correlation-weighted component. W_offdiag_shrink Numeric shrinkage factor applied -diagonal elements W_d. W_positive_diagonal_only Logical. TRUE, negative diagonal entries W_d set zero. W_normalize Normalization strategy applied W_d. One "trace", "max_abs", "none". lower_is_better Optional character vector listing traits smaller values favorable. traits internally flipped objectives align direction. merge_outputs Logical. TRUE mode = "", DG QGSI outputs merged using compare_dg_and_qgsi(). compare_sort_by Sorting rule passed compare_dg_and_qgsi(). Determines merged comparison table ordered. debug Logical. TRUE, function prints progress diagnostic messages execution.

title: Run a full desired-gain index and desired-gain QGSI pipeline — run_dgsi_qgsi_pipeline
text: list may contain: dg_result Output run_desired_gain_index_Joukhadar2024(). W_d_result Output construct_Wd_from_dg() quadratic matrix constructed automatically. qgsi_result Output run_qgsi_desired_gain(). comparison_result Output compare_dg_and_qgsi() workflows run merge_outputs = TRUE.

title: Run a full desired-gain index and desired-gain QGSI pipeline — run_dgsi_qgsi_pipeline
text: pipeline can operate three modes controlled mode: "dg" Runs desired-gain selection index workflow using run_desired_gain_index_Joukhadar2024(). "qgsi" Runs desired-gain QGSI workflow using run_qgsi_desired_gain(). W_d provided, matrix automatically constructed using construct_Wd_from_dg(). "" Runs workflows sequentially. merge_outputs = TRUE, results merged using compare_dg_and_qgsi() produce genotype-level comparison table. typical pipeline sequence mode = "" : run desired-gain index optimization; optionally construct quadratic desired-gain matrix W_d; compute QGSI scores using GEBVs; optionally merge outputs comparison table. wrapper particularly useful genomic selection pipelines breeders want compare: classical linear desired-gain indices, nonlinear quadratic genomic selection indices, resulting genotype rankings.

title: Run a full desired-gain index and desired-gain QGSI pipeline — run_dgsi_qgsi_pipeline
text: Joukhadar R et al. (2024). Optimising desired gain indices maximise selection response. Frontiers Plant Science 15:1337388. Cerón-Rojas JJ et al. (2026). Nonlinear genomic selection index accelerates multi-trait crop improvement. Nature Communications 17:1991.

title: Run a full desired-gain index and desired-gain QGSI pipeline — run_dgsi_qgsi_pipeline
code: trait_cols <- c("YLD", "MY", "MI", "BL", "NBL", "VHB")  dg <- c(   YLD = 1.5,   MY  = 0.5,   MI  = 0.5,   BL  = 1.0,   NBL = 1.0,   VHB = 1.0 )  ext <- system.file("extdata", package = "DGQGSI") pheno <- data.table::fread(file.path(ext, "example_pheno.csv")) gebv  <- data.table::fread(file.path(ext, "example_gebv.csv"))  res <- run_dgsi_qgsi_pipeline(   mode = "both",   init_data = pheno[, .(GenoID, Family)],   cand_data = pheno[, c("GenoID", trait_cols), with = FALSE],   gebv_data = gebv[, c("GenoID", trait_cols), with = FALSE],   trait_cols = trait_cols,   dg = dg,   lower_is_better = c("BL", "NBL", "VHB"),   merge_outputs = TRUE,   debug = FALSE )  head(res$comparison_result$comparison_table) #>    GenoID DG_SelectionIndex DG_Selected LinearDGPart QuadraticDGPart  QGSI_DG #>    <char>             <num>      <lgcl>        <num>           <num>    <num> #> 1:   G013          9.095305        TRUE     3.043771       0.8199342 3.863705 #> 2:   G016          7.375457        TRUE     2.934136       0.7698778 3.704014 #> 3:   G029          7.332733        TRUE     3.245327       0.9101123 4.155439 #> 4:   G001          6.995980        TRUE     2.581310       0.4790381 3.060348 #> 5:   G007          6.072997        TRUE     1.752643       0.2413784 1.994021 #> 6:   G025          5.851942        TRUE     1.849823       1.1382887 2.988112 #>    Rank_QGSI_DG SignSame_DG_QGSI #>           <num>           <lgcl> #> 1:            2             TRUE #> 2:            3             TRUE #> 3:            1             TRUE #> 4:            4             TRUE #> 5:            7             TRUE #> 6:            5             TRUE

what: Desired-gain QGSI using trait GEBVs — run_qgsi_desired_gain
title: Desired-gain QGSI using trait GEBVs — run_qgsi_desired_gain
text: Builds genomic quadratic selection index genotypes using QGSI structure proposed nonlinear genomic selection, replacing linear economic-weight vector breeder-defined desired-gain vector. implemented index : $$QGSI\_DG_i = d' \hat{\gamma}_i + \hat{\gamma}_i' W_d \hat{\gamma}_i$$ : \(d\) desired-gain vector, \(\hat{\gamma}_i\) vector trait GEBVs genotype \(\), \(W_d\) quadratic desired-gain matrix controlling trait--trait interaction contributions.

title: Desired-gain QGSI using trait GEBVs — run_qgsi_desired_gain
code: run_qgsi_desired_gain(   init_data,   gebv_data,   trait_cols,   id_col = "GenoID",   dg,   W_d = NULL,   quadratic_diag_weights = NULL,   lower_is_better = NULL,   center_traits = TRUE,   scale_traits = TRUE,   impute_missing = TRUE,   return_components = TRUE,   debug = TRUE )

title: Desired-gain QGSI using trait GEBVs — run_qgsi_desired_gain
text: init_data data.frame data.table containing genotype identifiers metadata preserve final output. Typical columns may include GenoID, family, population, descriptors. returned table appends QGSI results object. gebv_data data.frame data.table containing genotype IDs trait GEBVs. must contain id_col columns listed trait_cols. row represent one genotype trait column numeric coercible numeric. trait_cols Character vector giving trait GEBV column names use index. names must present gebv_data. order trait_cols determines order used dg, W_d, diagonal weights. id_col Character string naming genotype identifier column used align init_data gebv_data. Default "GenoID". column must present input tables. dg Named numeric desired-gain vector, names matching trait_cols. replaces classical economic-weight vector linear part index. best interpretability, dg scale transformed GEBV matrix used function. W_d Optional full symmetric quadratic desired-gain matrix dimension length(trait_cols) x length(trait_cols). matrix controls quadratic interaction contributions among traits. NULL, diagonal matrix created quadratic_diag_weights. quadratic_diag_weights Optional named numeric vector diagonal weights used W_d = NULL. Names must match trait_cols. Larger values place stronger emphasis quadratic contribution corresponding trait. lower_is_better Optional character vector naming traits smaller values favorable. traits internally multiplied -1 traits aligned common favorable direction building index. center_traits Logical. TRUE, GEBV matrix centered trait trait using column means index computed. useful GEBVs already centered around zero. scale_traits Logical. TRUE, trait column divided standard deviation optional centering. useful GEBVs different scales want standardized trait space. impute_missing Logical. TRUE, missing trait values imputed mean corresponding trait column. FALSE, function stops error missing values present. return_components Logical. TRUE, returned ranked table includes separate linear quadratic components addition final QGSI score. FALSE, final QGSI score rank retained. debug Logical. TRUE, function prints progress diagnostic messages execution. useful tracing data preparation matrix construction steps.

title: Desired-gain QGSI using trait GEBVs — run_qgsi_desired_gain
text: list containing: dg desired-gain vector used linear component. W_d quadratic desired-gain matrix used quadratic component. trait_space_used data.table containing transformed GEBV matrix actually used index calculation optional flipping, centering, scaling. ranked_geno data.table containing init_data augmented QGSI_DG, Rank_QGSI_DG, optionally LinearDGPart, QuadraticDGPart, Rank_LinearDGPart, Rank_QuadraticDGPart, sorted decreasing QGSI_DG. component_summary data.table summarizing mean, standard deviation, minimum, maximum computed index components.

title: Desired-gain QGSI using trait GEBVs — run_qgsi_desired_gain
text: function intended genomic selection workflows trait GEBVs already available genotype. function: aligns init_data gebv_data id_col; converts requested trait columns numeric; optionally imputes missing values trait means; optionally flips traits listed lower_is_better traits expressed common favorable direction; optionally centers /scales trait GEBVs; computes linear desired-gain component; computes quadratic interaction component using W_d; combines parts final desired-gain QGSI score; ranks genotypes resulting index. traits already standardized dg already expressed SD units, recommended settings : center_traits = FALSE scale_traits = FALSE trait GEBVs desired gains already scale. W_d supplied, function builds diagonal matrix quadratic_diag_weights. case, quadratic contribution captures squared trait effects, without -diagonal trait interactions.

title: Desired-gain QGSI using trait GEBVs — run_qgsi_desired_gain
text: Cerón-Rojas JJ, Montesinos-López OA, Montesinos-López , et al. (2026). Nonlinear genomic selection index accelerates multi-trait crop improvement. Nature Communications, 17:1991. doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69890-3

title: Desired-gain QGSI using trait GEBVs — run_qgsi_desired_gain
code: trait_cols <- c("YLD", "MY", "MI", "BL", "NBL", "VHB")  dg <- c(   YLD = 1.5,   MY  = 0.5,   MI  = 0.5,   BL  = 1.0,   NBL = 1.0,   VHB = 1.0 )  qdiag <- c(   YLD = 2.0,   MY  = 1.0,   MI  = 1.0,   BL  = 1.5,   NBL = 1.5,   VHB = 1.5 )  ext <- system.file("extdata", package = "DGQGSI") pheno <- data.table::fread(file.path(ext, "example_pheno.csv")) gebv  <- data.table::fread(file.path(ext, "example_gebv.csv"))  res <- run_qgsi_desired_gain(   init_data = pheno[, .(GenoID, Family)],   gebv_data = gebv[, c("GenoID", trait_cols), with = FALSE],   trait_cols = trait_cols,   dg = dg,   quadratic_diag_weights = qdiag,   lower_is_better = c("BL", "NBL", "VHB"),   center_traits = FALSE,   scale_traits = FALSE,   impute_missing = TRUE,   return_components = TRUE,   debug = FALSE )  head(res$ranked_geno) #>    GenoID Family LinearDGPart QuadraticDGPart   QGSI_DG Rank_QGSI_DG #>    <char> <char>        <num>           <num>     <num>        <num> #> 1:   G025     F5     1.849823        8.276079 10.125902            1 #> 2:   G029     F4     3.245327        5.339413  8.584740            2 #> 3:   G016     F1     2.934136        4.459293  7.393430            3 #> 4:   G013     F3     3.043771        4.127950  7.171721            4 #> 5:   G001     F1     2.581310        2.076364  4.657674            5 #> 6:   G015     F5     1.281385        3.313659  4.595043            6 #>    Rank_LinearDGPart Rank_QuadraticDGPart #>                <num>                <num> #> 1:                 6                    1 #> 2:                 1                    2 #> 3:                 3                    4 #> 4:                 2                    6 #> 5:                 4                   17 #> 6:                 9                    9 res$component_summary #>          Component      Mean       SD        Min       Max #>             <char>     <num>    <num>      <num>     <num> #> 1:    LinearDGPart 0.5528237 1.448067 -2.6519349  3.245327 #> 2: QuadraticDGPart 2.6898094 1.628205  0.4977952  8.276079 #> 3:         QGSI_DG 3.2426331 2.361725  0.2290951 10.125902

title: DGQGSI 0.1.0
text: Initial package structure. Added desired-gain selection index workflow. Added desired-gain QGSI workflow. Added automatic W_d construction helper. Added comparison helper wrapper pipeline. Added pkgdown GitHub Actions configuration.
